page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration includes 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Memory capacity stands at 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 1800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the m5dn.12xlarge consistently in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance registers at 40,670.61, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 11,131.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block speed of 2,252.6 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 47.91 GB/sec. In database operations, the instance achieves a PassMark database score of 15,484.29 and processes 37.85 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-threaded performance is a relative bottleneck, with single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 25% tier at 23.35 GB/sec. Large LLM inference is also limited, with 70B model prompt processing yielding a weak 5.75 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5dn.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density, combining high-speed local NVMe storage and substantial network throughput with standard general-purpose compute resources. This makes it highly suitable for workloads requiring a blend of fast local storage and high network performance, such as relational databases, distributed data stores, static web serving, and moderate-scale data processing. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference due to the observed performance tradeoffs in those areas.
description: The Amazon Web Services m5dn.12xlarge is a Nitro-based general-purpose instance designed for network and EBS-optimized workloads. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz, delivering 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate solid multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,131.0 and 37.85 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-threaded tasks like gzip compression show weaker relative performance. This instance is well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and applications requiring high network throughput combined with fast local storage.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform combining high-speed local NVMe storage with robust multi-core performance and high-bandwidth networking.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB DDR4 RAM
1800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for data-intensive transfers
Strong multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads
Tradeoffs in single-threaded tasks and large LLM inference
